page: Microsoft Azure E2d_v4 is a memory-optimized virtual machine belonging to the EDv4 family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features an x86_64 architecture powered by a dedicated Intel Xeon 8272CL processor operating at 2.6 GHz. This configuration provides 2 vCPUs derived from 1 physical core with hyperthreading enabled, resulting in 2 threads per core. The processor includes 32 KB of L1d cache, 1.0 MB of L2 cache, and 35 MB of L3 cache, alongside support for AVX, AVX512F, and AVX512_VNNI instruction sets.

The server is equipped with 16.0 GB of system memory, yielding a high memory-to-core ratio of 8.0 GB per core. It also includes 77 GB of bundled local storage. There are no GPUs or complimentary public IPv4 addresses included with this instance type.

Performance benchmarks indicate a clear divergence between single-core and multi-core capabilities. Single-core performance is moderate, with stress-ng single-core CPU performance and Geekbench single-core scores falling in the average category. Conversely, multi-core and memory bandwidth metrics are limited. Multi-core CPU performance in stress-ng, Geekbench multi-core scores, and Passmark CPU scores all rank in the bottom 10%. Memory bandwidth tests show low throughput, with read-only large block bandwidth at 14.22 GB/sec and write large block bandwidth at 11.16 GB/sec. Database operations and Redis SET operations also register in the bottom 10% tier.

From a qualitative cost-efficiency perspective, the E2d_v4 offers a high memory-to-vCPU density suitable for memory-bound workloads that do not require intensive parallel processing. Due to the low multi-core and memory bandwidth performance, it is appropriate for lightweight database caching, small-scale web serving, single-threaded file processing, or basic memory-intensive development tasks rather than high-throughput parallel computing.
description: Microsoft Azure E2d_v4 is an x86_64 memory-optimized virtual machine from the EDv4 family, running on Microsoft Hyper-V. It features a dedicated Intel Xeon 8272CL processor with 2 vCPUs and 2.6 GHz clock speed, 16.0 GB of RAM, and 77 GB of local storage. While single-core benchmarks fall in the average range, multi-core and memory bandwidth performance are positioned in the bottom 10%. The instance lacks GPU resources and complimentary public IPv4 addresses. Qualitatively, this server provides high memory density relative to its compute capacity, making it a cost-efficient option for memory-bound workloads with low parallel processing demands. It is appropriate for lightweight database caching, single-threaded processing, and small-scale web serving.

tagline: A memory-optimized virtual machine featuring a high memory-to-vCPU ratio and bundled local storage for lightweight, memory-bound workloads.

16.0 GB of system memory providing a high 8.0 GB per core ratio
77 GB of bundled local storage for temporary data requirements
Average single-core performance contrasted with lower multi-core efficiency
Appropriate for lightweight database caching and single-threaded tasks
